name: Progressive Ozark Bank
description_short: Community bank operating since 1965 in the Ozark region of Missouri, offering checking, savings, student accounts, and local lending with six branch locations.
description_long:
Progressive Ozark Bank is a locally-owned community bank that has been serving the Ozark region of Missouri since 1965. The bank emphasizes its deep roots in the community and positions itself as an alternative to larger national institutions by highlighting its local decision-making processes and personal service model.

The bank offers a range of retail banking services including checking and savings accounts, student debit card accounts with no overdraft fees, online and mobile banking with instant money transfer capabilities, and loan products for home, auto, and personal purposes. They also serve agricultural and small business customers with specialized lending programs. The institution provides both consumer and business online banking platforms (BusinessPRO) and maintains six physical branch locations across Missouri (Salem, Houston, Hartville, Licking, Mountain Grove, and Mansfield).

Progressive Ozark Bank distinguishes itself through its emphasis on local loan approval decisions made by in-house staff rather than remote underwriting, positioning this as a key differentiator from larger national banks. The bank actively engages in community programs—including recognition of Community Banking Month and involvement in local high school initiatives. Their marketing emphasizes personalized service, flexibility in checking options, and a commitment to understanding customers' individual needs and local business requirements.

As a small regional bank, Progressive Ozark Bank serves customers who prioritize local banking relationships and community connection over digital-only banking or branch convenience. The primary limitation is geographic availability, with operations confined to Missouri's Ozark region. While they offer modern digital tools and mobile banking, their competitive advantages rest primarily on relationship banking rather than product innovation or pricing leadership. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Student debit card accounts with no overdraft fees
Mobile wallet and instant money transfer
Online banking platform for personal customers
Home loans with local approval decisions
Auto loans with local approval decisions
Personal loans with local approval decisions
Mobile banking app with activity alerts
pros: No overdraft fees on student debit card accounts, reducing unexpected charges for young account holders
Lending decisions made locally by in-house staff rather than remote underwriting, enabling faster approval and personalized consideration
Six branch locations across Missouri Ozark region (Salem, Houston, Hartville, Licking, Mountain Grove, Mansfield) for in-person banking
Mobile app with instant money transfer and activity alerts for managing accounts on-the-go
Specialized lending products for farm and agriculture, home and real estate, and small business customers
Community-focused institution emphasizing local relationships and personalized service since 1965
Online and mobile banking platforms available 24/7 for both personal and business customers
cons: Geographic limitation: Only operates in Missouri's Ozark region, unavailable to customers outside these six specific locations
No information provided on specific APR rates, fees, or competitive pricing for checking, savings, or loan products
Limited details on deposit insurance coverage, FDIC membership, or other regulatory protections on website
No mention of investment services, wealth management, or financial planning beyond basic lending and deposit products
Website lacks transparency on business hours for all locations and does not clearly display current interest rates or account requirements

contact.address: 904 W Scenic Rivers Blvd, Salem, MO 65560

best_for: Rural Missouri residents in the Ozark region seeking personalized banking relationships and local loan decision-making
Small business owners and farmers in the service area who benefit from specialized lending programs and local understanding
Students and families prioritizing no-fee checking accounts with mobile banking convenience
Customers who value community banking and personal service over larger national bank chains
diagnosis: Progressive Ozark Bank is best for rural Missouri residents and small business owners in the Ozark region who prioritize personal relationships and local lending decisions over the convenience of large branch networks or competitive rates. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation—the bank only serves customers in its six Missouri locations, making it unsuitable for those outside the service area or those seeking broader banking access.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 904 W Scenic Rivers Blvd, Salem, MO 65560
